response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said brings 15+ years of broad engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and backend systems, with demonstrated depth in TypeScript/Node.js and credible high-throughput systems work at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby. However, the role is explicitly built around experimentation platforms, A/B testing frameworks, feature flagging, telemetry infrastructure, and statistical analysis — none of which appear anywhere in the resume. The candidate operates at Staff/Senior level with no evidence of the Principal/IC6-level cross-team architectural ownership, growth measurement systems, or data-driven experimentation leadership the position requires.
strengths: Proven high-throughput, low-latency backend experience handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby using micro-services and async I/O
Recent AI-agent infrastructure and developer tooling work at re:cinq (MCP frameworks, agentic workflows) aligns with the company's AI-first engineering direction
Long track record of self-directed technical initiative: open source platform ownership, conference talks, workshop facilitation, and multi-language versatility
concerns: Zero evidence of experimentation platforms, A/B testing frameworks, feature flagging, rollout automation, or bias detection — the primary technical domain of the role
No telemetry infrastructure, metrics pipeline, or statistical/experimental analysis experience mentioned anywhere in the resume
Current seniority (Staff/Senior IC) and career history show no cross-team architectural leadership, growth strategy ownership, or multi-workstream program execution at Principal/IC6 scale

key_factors.factor: No experimentation or growth systems experience
No telemetry or metrics infrastructure background
